briefdescription.para: This function does a minimal test on the timer counter device and the low level driver as a design example.

detaileddescription.para: The purpose of this function is to illustrate how to use the XTtcPs low level driver.

SettingsTableOffset
is an offset into the settings table. This allows multiple counter setups to be kept and swapped easily.
XST_SUCCESS to indicate success, else XST_FAILURE to indicate a Failure.
This function contains a loop which waits for the value of a timer counter to change. If the hardware is not working correctly, this function may not return.

briefdescription.para: This function is the main function of the Timer/Counter example.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS to indicate success, else XST_FAILURE to indicate a Failure.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a design example using the Triple Timer Counter hardware and driver in polled mode.

detaileddescription.para: The example generates a square wave output on the waveform out pin.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 drg/jz 01/23/10 First release 3.0 pkp 12/09/14 Change TTC_NUM_DEVICES for Zynq Ultrascale MP support 3.9 mus 04/09/19 Updated SettingsTable values as per TmrCntrSetup template 3.18 dp 05/23/23 Update checks for Intervalvalue and Matchvalue based on platform. 3.18 ml 03/06/24 Added support for system device-tree flow. 3.21 vmt 03/28/25 Fixed signedness warning by explicitly casting to u8. 3.22 ml 07/14/25 Fixed GCC warnings.
